free_text: Elastic scattering and radiative capture of protons
by 13C
CNPD: corrected 4-dig. year and lower cases
SD: Corrections in all Subents. FACILITY,
SAMPLE, DETECTOR moved to Subents 002,003. Subents
SD: Correction in all Subents.
600-keV for gamma-ray measurements
The target for the radiative
capture experiments was prepared from methyl iodide
enriched to 61% 13C. The 0.5 mm thick tantalum target
blank was coated with a layer of approximately
20 ug/cm2 of enriched carbon, after cleaning and
outgassing.
10.2 cm x 10.2 cm NaI(Tl) crystal mounted on a
Dumont 6364 photomultiplier.
The non-resonant distributions from the close
neighborhood of the 448-keV resonance were found to
be isotropic, within experimental errors.
No information about source of uncertainty
Tbl. 2
SD: SF8=LEG/RSD -> LEG/RS; SF9=EXP deleted
from REACTION code. FACILITY, SAMPLE, DETECTOR added.
ERR-S -> DATA-ERR. COMMENT added. E-LVL-INI=7.97 MeV
deleted (equivalent to EN=0.448 MeV). E-LVL-FIN->E-LVL.
SD: STATUS updated.
No information about source of uncertainty
1-MV for elastic scattering
The targets used for the elastic
scattering experiments were prepared from methyl
iodide enriched to 66% 13C This enriched methyl
iodide was decomposed on red-hot tantalum blanks that
previously been coated with layer of natural carbon
approximately 300 ug/cm2 thick. The layer of enriched
carbon had thickness of approximately 50 ug/cm2
(15keV at Ep=600 keV) .
double-focusing mag. spectrometer

EN-ERR -> EN-ERR-DIG;DATA-ERR1 -> ERR-DIG;
DATA-ERR2 -> DATA-ERR.  MB -> MB/SR.
SD: STATUS updated.
600-keV for gamma-ray measurements
The target was 9.0 keV thick for
448-keV protons, and was enriched to 61% C13.
10.2 cm x 10.2 cm NaI(Tl) crystal mounted on a
Dumont 6364 photomultiplier.
Statistical uncertainty. In many cases less
than size of the point on fig.

600-keV for gamma-ray measurements
The cross section was renormalized to make it agree
with the calculated resonance cross section
Two targets were used.
1. A target of 39% C12 with a thickness of 9.0 keV
for 448-keV protons was used.
2. A natural carbon target, approximately 20 keV
thick was used.
Natural carbon
Enriched to 61% C-13.
The results from the natural target were normalized
in the overlapping range to agree with the results
from the target of known thickness, taken at higher
energies.
10.2 cm x 10.2 cm NaI(Tl) crystal mounted on a
Dumont 6364 photomultiplier.

that size of he point on fig.
